title: Business Development - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search 24 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ACCENT NETWORK BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides business development services such as business plan workshops, record keeping, and other assistance for starting or growing a business. 12254 SW Garden Place Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers with a focus on Russian and Ukrainian population Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-8pm Email (971) 280-6738 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADELANTE MUJERES MICROENTERPRISE PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides microenterprise programs that include: -Empresas Small Business Development Program: Provides training, mentoring, and referrals. Individual development accounts (IDAs) and financial literacy classes are also offered. Provides access to a commercial kitchen for small catering and food product businesses. -Sustainable Agriculture Program, Community-Supported Agriculture (CSA), and Distributor: Provides training on organic farming and marketing support. Connects Latino growers to local eaters and matches produce to institutional buyers. IDAs are also available. -Forest Grove and Cornelius Farmers Markets: Serves as a small-business incubator for the Empresas and Sustainable Agriculture programs. 2030 Main Street Suite A Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Low-income Latino community members Hours: Office hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Program hours include evenings and weekends Email (503) 992-0078 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND PROSPER PORTLAND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers economic development programs that support small business, improve access to workforce training, and create jobs for Portland residents. 220 NW 2nd Avenue Suite 200 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 823-3200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY COLLEGE SMALL BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential one-on-one business advising to new and existing businesses through personalized training and workshops. Assists with business and marketing plans, financial proposals, and provides market research assistance. 1651 Lexington Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Current or prospective for-profit business owners Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 338-2402 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEVNW DEVNW CORVALLIS COMMUNITY LENDINGWORKS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides small loans to people and businesses. Includes auto, consumer, credit-builder, micro, and small business loans. Also offers affordable housing finance and small business equity investments. 2525 SE Third Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 345-0446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEVNW DEVNW OREGON CITY COMMUNITY LENDINGWORKS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides small loans to people and businesses. Includes auto, consumer, credit-builder, micro, and small business loans. Also offers affordable housing finance and small business equity investments. 421 High Street Suite 110 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 345-0446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEVNW DEVNW SALEM COMMUNITY LENDINGWORKS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides small loans to people and businesses. Includes auto, consumer, credit-builder, micro, and small business loans. Also offers affordable housing finance and small business equity investments. 528 Cottage Street NE Suite 1C Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 345-0446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEVNW DEVNW SPRINGFIELD COMMUNITY LENDINGWORKS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides small loans to people and businesses. Includes auto, consumer, credit-builder, micro, and small business loans. Also offers affordable housing finance and small business equity investments. 212 Main Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 345-0446 1 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HACIENDA COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION EMPRESARIOS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support to start or grow a business in the food industry. Offers monthly orientations, small business workshops, access to a commercial kitchen, capital, and connections to Portland’s network of entrepreneurial support. 6700 NE Killingsworth Street Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Unrestricted with a focus on Latino/a/e/x, immigrant, women, and minority-owned businesses Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (503) 459-7608 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides training and accreditation services to provide home-based child care, microenterprise development services, financial literacy training, and individual development accounts (IDAs). 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: child care, microenterprise development services, financial literacy training, and individual development accounts (IDAs). 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 271-6470 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOSEPHINE COUNTY FARM COLLECTIVE KITCHEN RENTAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a commercial food processing kitchen focused on smaller farms and market gardeners, with appliances for freeze drying, garlic peeling, and dehydrating. The kitchen does not have an oven, and a maximum of 10 people are allowed on site. 485 Daily Lane Grants Pass, OR 97527 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday/Thursday/Saturday/Sunday 8am-8pm, Friday limited hours may be available Email (530) 304-3754 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workforce development assistance including computer training, tutoring in English or ESL classes, application assistance, resume assistance, small business development workshops and advice, and information and referral for employment and training opportunities. 2680 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 110 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 10am-5pm, Tuesday 10am-7pm Email (541) 382-4366 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION MADRAS WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workforce development assistance including computer training, tutoring in English or ESL classes, application assistance, resume assistance, small business development workshops and advice, and information and referral for employment and training opportunities. 231 SE 6th Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-5pm or by appointment, Wednesday 10am-7pm Email (541) 598-6778 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION REDMOND WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workforce development assistance including computer training, tutoring in English or ESL classes, application assistance, resume assistance, small business development workshops and advice, and information and referral for employment and training opportunities. 636 NW Cedar Avenue Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-5pm or by appointment Email (541) 316-5456 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVELIHOOD NW OPEN BUSINESS ADVISING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity for people to access information and resources about running a business, including planning and strategy development, market research, human resources support, financial analysis, loan packaging, and specialized information and referral. It also offers a course focused specifically on assistance with accounting and bookkeeping systems. PO Box 13775 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Unrestricted, with a focus on individuals who identify as female, BIPOC, or LGBTQ2+ Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 988-8255 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER MICRO ENTERPRISE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides technical assistance, training in business skills, assistance writing business plans, access to financial business counseling, individual development accounts (IDAs), and other support for individuals who want to establish a small business. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as tribal members Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST NATIVE CHAMBER CULTURALLY SPECIFIC COMMUNITY ORGANIZATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information, networking opportunities, mentoring, and technical assistance for businesses. 6500 S Macadam Avenue Suite 175 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Native American community Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 894-4525 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST NATIVE CHAMBER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information, networking opportunities, mentoring, and technical assistance for businesses in areas of financial management, legal support, marketing, and more. Also offers a scholarship fund for American Indian and Alaskan Native students entering college. 6500 S Macadam Avenue Suite 175 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Business owners, entrepreneurs, and students within the Native American Indian and Alaskan Native communities Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 894-4525 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON ROHINGYA MYANMAR MUSLIM SOCIETY EMPLOYMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers employment and career services including job placement assistance, resume building, interview preparation, and career counseling. Also provides support in navigating the local job market, offering guidance on workers rights and workplace culture. 2410 SE 121st Avenue Suite #208 Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Must meet one of the following: -Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers -Low-income individuals and families residing in Oregon -Individuals with limited English proficiency -Families experiencing hardship, displacement, or housing instability -Youth, adults, and elders across all ages -BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, Persons of Color) -Rohingya and Myanmar Community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 489-5179 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON ROHINGYA MYANMAR MUSLIM SOCIETY AGRICULTURAL AND FARMING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides agricultural and farming support to help community members engage in sustainable farming practices and improve their livelihoods. This support includes connecting individuals to local agricultural resources, providing guidance on farming techniques, and offering assistance in starting small-scale farming or gardening projects. Also helps community members access training and tools necessary for success in Oregon's agricultural environment, fostering economic stability and self-sufficiency. 2410 SE 121st Avenue Suite #208 Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Must meet one of the following: -Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers -Low-income individuals and families residing in Oregon -Individuals with limited English proficiency -Families experiencing hardship, displacement, or housing instability -Youth, adults, and elders across all ages -BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, Persons of Color) -Rohingya and Myanmar Community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 489-5179 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEOPLE'S COLLECTIVE COMMUNITY CENTER FINANCIAL LITERACY CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial literacy classes. Business mentoring, including culturally sensitive supportive service and access to start-up grants, is available. Child care is available if requested. 1695 Jefferson Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted but with a focus on the African-American community Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 868-6337 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SCORE ASSOCIATION SCORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides business mentoring, webinars and courses on demand, an online resource library, and local business events for new and existing small business owners. 712 H Street NE Washington, DC 20002 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email (800) 634-0245 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WARM SPRINGS COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM SMALL BUSINESS SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides business education and one-on-one counseling and coaching. Topics may include; marketing, setting financial goals, developing business plans using Idea Buddy, business registration, learning about loan opportunities, including Kiva loans. 1136 Paiute Avenue Warm Springs, OR 97761 Eligibility: People who live, work, or have addresses on the Warm Springs Indian Reservation Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 553-1348 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY COOPERATIVE LIBRARY SERVICES JOB AND CAREER HELP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with job searching (online job coaching, practice interviewing, and feedback on resume), information on starting and operating a small bussiness, and online video courses taught by industry experts. Confidential Mailing Address Eligibility: Washington County library card holder Hours: All other services: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Job coaches: 7 days per week 1pm-10pm, closed holidays Email Visit Website More Details ACCENT NETWORK BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT ADELANTE MUJERES MICROENTERPRISE PROGRAMS CITY OF PORTLAND PROSPER PORTLAND CLATSOP COMMUNITY COLLEGE SMALL BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT CENTER DEVNW COMMUNITY LENDINGWORKS DEVNW COMMUNITY LENDINGWORKS DEVNW COMMUNITY LENDINGWORKS DEVNW COMMUNITY LENDINGWORKS HACIENDA COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION EMPRESARIOS IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT JOSEPHINE COUNTY FARM COLLECTIVE KITCHEN RENTAL LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM LIVELIHOOD NW OPEN BUSINESS ADVISING NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER MICRO ENTERPRISE NORTHWEST NATIVE CHAMBER CULTURALLY SPECIFIC COMMUNITY ORGANIZATIONS OREGON ROHINGYA MYANMAR MUSLIM SOCIETY EMPLOYMENT SERVICES PEOPLE'S COLLECTIVE COMMUNITY CENTER FINANCIAL LITERACY CLASSES WARM SPRINGS COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM SMALL BUSINESS SUPPORT
